title: Bitter Fluffy Turkish Cornmeal Pilaf
description: A traditional Turkish dish featuring bitter notes from kale and a fluffy texture from cornmeal, perfect for a nostalgic vegetarian meal. This pilaf is broiled to perfection, giving it a crispy top and a fluffy interior.
instructions: In a large pot, bring the water to a boil.
Add the cornmeal to the pot, stirring constantly to avoid lumps.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 10 minutes or until the cornmeal is tender.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the onion and garlic to the skillet, cooking until the onion is translucent.
Add the chopped kale to the skillet, cooking until wilted.
Stir the paprika, salt, and pepper into the skillet.
Combine the cooked cornmeal, skillet mixture, and vegetable broth in a large bowl.
Transfer the mixture to a baking dish and broil for 5-7 minutes or until the top is golden brown.
